structured_fields.name: prevalence
structured_fields.description: Global or regional prevalence of the disease — cases per population unit
structured_fields.example: 1 in 1,000 globally; higher in women of childbearing age

structured_fields.name: incidence_rate
structured_fields.description: Annual incidence rate — new cases per 100,000 population per year

structured_fields.name: geographic_distribution
structured_fields.description: Prevalence breakdown by region — used for bar chart rendering

structured_fields.name: demographics
structured_fields.description: Key demographic patterns associated with disease prevalence

ethnicity_notes.description: Ethnic or racial groups with notably higher or lower prevalence
ethnicity_notes.example: 3x higher prevalence in African American and Hispanic women vs white women

structured_fields.name: mortality_rate
structured_fields.description: Reported mortality rate or survival statistics
structured_fields.example: 10-year survival rate approximately 90% in high-income countries

narrative.description: Brief narrative on overall societal and economic burden
narrative.example: Lupus imposes significant economic burden through lost productivity and high treatment costs particularly in working-age women

charts.title: Prevalence by Region (per 100k)
charts.caption: Regional variation in disease prevalence per 100,000 population

prompt_hints.tone: precise and data-driven — cite ranges where exact figures vary
avoid: vague statements like 'affects millions worldwide' without figures
inventing DALYs or mortality figures — mark as unavailable if unknown
prefer: specific numeric values with population denominators
noting data source context where relevant (e.g. high-income vs low-income countries)
flagging where data is limited or contested
